story: Surf's up! On February 11, 2020, Brazilian surfer Maya Gabeira caught the ride of her life. At Nazaré, Portugal, she conquered a massive wave towering as tall as a building. story: Jack Holden's shoes fell apart during the Empire Games marathon in Auckland, but he didn't let that stop him! His incredible perseverance and quick thinking turned a potential disaster into a legendary win.

title: Pluto's Cosmic Dance with Neptune
story: Pluto pulled off a celestial surprise in 1999! On February 11, this icy world slipped past Neptune, becoming the farthest planet from the sun. title: Japan Joins the Space Race
story: Blast off! On February 11, 1970, Japan rocketed into the space age. The country launched Ohsumi, its first satellite, becoming only the fourth nation to put an object in orbit around Earth. title: Shuttle Discovery's Historic Landing
story: Whoosh! The Space Shuttle Discovery glided to a smooth landing on February 11, 1994. This wasn't just any mission - it was STS-60, the first to include a Russian cosmonaut on an American spacecraft. The crew had spent over a week in orbit, conducting experiments and proving that former rivals could work together high above Earth. It marked a new era of space cooperation!
activity_question: What made the STS-60 mission special?
activity_choices: It was the first to include a Russian cosmonaut
It was the longest shuttle mission ever
It carried the first space penguin
activity_answer: It was the first to include a Russian cosmonaut
follow_up_question: Why do you think it's important for countries to work together in space?

In 1895, this Scottish village hit an icy -17°F, cold enough to freeze your socks off! Brave residents bundled up, sipped hot cocoa, and huddled by roaring fires to stay warm. February 11 became a frosty legend, reminding us how tough Scottish winters can be.
activity_question: What was the record-breaking temperature in Braemar?
follow_up_question: How do you think people stayed warm on such a freezing day?

title: Laughs and Awards: TV's Big Night
story: Lights, camera, laughter! The 6th Emmy Awards in 1954 celebrated the best of television. On February 11, beloved shows and stars took home golden trophies. The hilarious 'I Love Lucy' won big, making audiences giggle even more. Funny man Donald O'Connor and witty Eve Arden also snagged awards for their comedic talents. It was a night of glitz and giggles!
activity_question: Which popular TV show won an Emmy Award in 1954?
follow_up_question: Do you know any other famous TV shows from the 1950s?

title: Breaking Barriers in the White House
story: Robert Weaver made history in 1961. On February 11, he became the first African American to hold a cabinet-level position in the United States government. President Kennedy appointed Weaver as the Administrator of the Housing and Home Finance Agency. This groundbreaking moment opened doors for more diverse leadership in the highest levels of American politics.
activity_question: What position did Robert Weaver take in 1961?
activity_choices: Administrator of the Housing and Home Finance Agency
Chief Justice of the Supreme Court
activity_answer: Administrator of the Housing and Home Finance Agency
follow_up_question: Why do you think it's important to have diverse leadership in government?

title: Breaking Barriers in the US Army
story: Clifford Alexander Jr. shattered a glass ceiling in 1976. On February 11, he became the first African American to lead the US Army as Secretary. This historic appointment opened doors for greater diversity in military leadership. Alexander's confirmation marked a significant step towards equal representation in the highest ranks of the armed forces.
activity_question: What position did Clifford Alexander Jr. become the first African American to hold?
follow_up_question: Why do you think diversity in leadership is important?

title: Challenger's Cosmic Comeback
story: Whoosh! The space shuttle Challenger zoomed back to Earth, completing its fourth mission. This cosmic journey marked NASA's 10th shuttle flight overall. In 1984, on February 11, brave astronauts guided the massive spacecraft through a fiery re-entry, landing safely after days of orbiting our planet. Their return brought new knowledge about space travel and life among the stars.
activity_question: What was the name of the space shuttle that returned to Earth?
follow_up_question: What do you think astronauts do during their time in space?

title: Discovery's Daring Dance in Space
story: Swooping through the stars, the space shuttle Discovery completed its 19th mission in 1995. On February 11, after days of cosmic exploration, the brave astronauts guided their spacecraft back to Earth. As Discovery touched down, it marked another successful journey in NASA's ongoing quest to unlock the mysteries of the universe.

title: Cricket Clash: West Indies' Mega Score
story: Bats were swinging and runs were piling up! In 1995, the West Indies cricket team unleashed a batting storm against New Zealand. On February 11, they racked up an incredible 5-660 score in a Test match. That's like scoring over a hundred goals in soccer! The Kiwis must have felt overwhelmed by this cricket tsunami.
activity_question: What sport were the West Indies and New Zealand playing?
follow_up_question: Do you know what 'Test match' means in cricket?
